Basant Panchami, Saraswati Puja

# Basant Panchami, Saraswati Puja

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jan 23, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

10

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Basant Panchami, traditionally refers to the fifth day of the month of Magh, usually around January – February. It is primarily meant to welcome the arrival of Spring, after the long and dreary winter in India. This is observed primarily in the northern and eastern parts of India, not much in the southern or western parts. Technically speaking, it falls in Magha Shukla Panchami, the bright phase of the moon. Magha in a sense, marks the spring festival cycle in India, that also includes, Ratha Saptami, Maha Shivratri and concludes with Holi.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!shC9!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fefa239e0-bcfb-4818-9f7b-7be5ae3eea21_650x540.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!shC9!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fefa239e0-bcfb-4818-9f7b-7be5ae3eea21_650x540.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**Ancient India**

In ancient times, Vasant Panchami was celebrated in honor of the God of love, Kamadev and his wife Rati. Spring or Vasant was Kamadev’s close friend, and hence it was associated with him. It was a celebration of Shringara, where dancing girls, dressed specially in a saffron sari with red dots, would come to the palace, and mingle with the royals.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MkEb!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F554ebfbd-e822-4584-be22-fdfd63829cb2_220x263.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!MkEb!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F554ebfbd-e822-4584-be22-fdfd63829cb2_220x263.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

They would sing various songs on love, primarily on Radha Krishna. At the end of the festivities, the flowers and mango leaves would be sprinkled with red gulal, which these girls would apply to their cheeks.One interesting aspect, there is a gap of 40 days between Vasant Panchami and Holi. It is believed to be the number of days, Rati undertook penance after her husband Kamadeva was burnt to ashes, by Shiva’s third eye, when he shot the love arrows. In places like Baithan, the sprinkling of color is done on Vasant Panchami and it continues till Holi.

Basant Panchami is also celebrated by Sufi Muslims since 12th century AD. It is believed that the poet Amir Khusro, dressed in yellow, and took flowers to the saint Nizamuddin, who was mourning the death of his nephew. This made him feel better and it is celebrated at the Nizamuddin Dargah as well as all those belonging to the Chisti order.

While both Shiva and Vishnu are worshiped on this day, it is only the latter who wear the yellow colored clothing that is a theme of this festival. Another interesting celebration is in Aurangabad district of Bihar, where prayers are offered at the Deo shrine, in honor of the Surya there. In Uttarakhand this is also a harvest festival, where earthen lamps and incense are lit, to signify worship of the earth. While people in Bihar and Bengal, worship the plough on this date.

**Saraswati Puja**

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ckOo!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9f747835-d9ec-4c1e-9274-198a28e49081_220x356.jpeg)

![Saraswati.jpg](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ckOo!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9f747835-d9ec-4c1e-9274-198a28e49081_220x356.jpeg "Saraswati.jpg")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The major celebration on this day though is the Saraswati Puja. An interesting point to note, that in Indian culture, we have three goddesses representing, Wealth( Lakshmi), Power( Shakti) and Education( Saraswati). That shows the importance we lay on all the 3 aspects. Saraswati is generally believed to have been derived from a combination of the Sanskrit words Sara, meaning essence, and Sva meaning self. So it translates basically into “essence of one’s own self”. She is first mentioned in the Rigveda both as a river as well as a goddess.

> > *अम्बितमे नदीतमे देवितमे **सरस्वति** \|*
>
> > *Best Mother, best of Rivers, best of Goddesses, Sarasvatī, We are,
> > as of no repute and dear Mother, give thou us renown.*

She is also celebrated as a deity with the powers of healing, purifying powers of abundant, flowing waters in Rigveda.

> > *अपो अस्मान मातरः शुन्धयन्तु घर्तेन नो घर्तप्वः पुनन्तु \|  
> > विश्वं हि रिप्रं परवहन्ति देविरुदिदाभ्यः शुचिरापूत एमि \|\|*

It translates to *“May the waters, may the mother cleanse us, may they who purify, purify us with butter, I come up out of them, pure and cleansed”.*

Many are her names, Brahmani( Goddess of Science), Vani( referring to the flow of music). In Telugu she is referred to as Chaduvula Thalli( Goddess of education). Her other names include Veenapani, Vidya, Pustaka Dharini, Vagdevi. In Tamil she is referred to as Kalaimagal, Kalaivani. A very common name is Sharada, one who loves the Autumn season.

It is not just in India, she is also worshiped in other countries too, mostly in Asia. In Myanmar she is referred to as Thurathadi, and is considered the Goddess of learning much like in India. The Japanese counterpart of Saraswati is Benzaiten, and she holds the biwa, a traditional Japanese lute, much like Saraswati holds the Veena. In Japan she is the Goddess for everything that flows: water, knowledge, speech, music, time.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eY6b!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8c3e74f2-63df-49a5-bcc5-2672b2bfad3f_220x293.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!eY6b!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8c3e74f2-63df-49a5-bcc5-2672b2bfad3f_220x293.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

In Cambodia she is referred to as Vagisvari or Bharti, while in Thailand she is called as Suratswadi. She is also an important goddess in Bali, and is also considered the Goddess of learning, pretty much like in India again. Saraswati Puja is one of the major festivals in Bali, celebrated by Hindus mostly, where people offer flowers to her.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ssN4!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F84e0aa4c-0eef-4d69-b04d-46b255876d7d_220x292.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ssN4!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F84e0aa4c-0eef-4d69-b04d-46b255876d7d_220x292.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

**Symbolism**

Saraswati is seated on a white lotus, that symbolizes, knowledge, truth and light. She is often depicted in white, the color representing Sattwa Guna, symbolizing purity and wisdom. Her four hands often depicted symbolize, *manas( sense), buddhi( intellect), chitta( imagination) and ahamkar( self consciousness).* Each of these 4 hands again items that have their own symbolic meaning. The crystal mala in one hand represents the power of meditation and spirituality, the pot of water, the ability to differentiate between right and wrong. The Veena, represents all the creative arts and sciences, and her holding represents the harmony created by knowledge. Her mount is a white swan. It is believed that a swan has the ability to differentiate between milk and water, and so it symbolically represents the wisdom to think and reason. The swan also symbolizes spiritual perfection, transcendence and moksha. More often than not she is depicted as being near a river, which could point back to her early history as a river goddess.

**Saraswati Puja**

As per the Brahma Purana, Saraswati was granted a boon by Shri Krishna, that she shall be worshiped on Vasant Panchami day. Traditionally a Kalash containing water, is set up, after worshiping Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, and then colored powder is thrown into the air to mark the occasion. Many wear yellow color to symbolize the mustard flowers that bloom during spring time.During Basant Panchami celebrations in Bengal, Odisha, Saraswathi is worshiped in a yellow dress, symbolizing the mustard flowers blooming.People celebrating Basant Panchami in East, generally wear yellow color garments, deck up Saraswati’s idol too in yellow. In the Telugu states , Basant Panchami is observed with prayers to Maa Saraswati, and also observing Akshara Abhyasam or Vidya Arambham.

**Saraswati Vandana**

> > *या कुंदेंदु तुषारहार धवला, या शुभ्र वस्त्रावृता \|*
>
> > *या वीणावर दण्डमंडितकरा, या श्वेतपद्मासना \|\|*
>
> > *या ब्रह्माच्युतशंकरप्रभ्रृतिभिर्देवै: सदा वन्दिता \|*
>
> > *सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेष जाड्यापहा \|\|*
>
> > *शुक्लां ब्रह्मविचार सार परमां आद्यां जगद्व्यापिनीं*
>
> > *वीणा पुस्तक धारिणीं अभयदां जाड्यान्धकारापाहां\|*
>
> > *हस्ते स्फाटिक मालीकां विदधतीं पद्मासने संस्थितां*
>
> > *वन्दे तां परमेश्वरीं भगवतीं बुद्धि प्रदां शारदां\|\|  *

The prayer if one interprets it, is this way

Yaa kundendu, tushara haara dhavala, – As Fair as the Kunda flower, white as the moon and garland of tushara flowers

Yaa Shubra Vastra Avitaa- Covered in white clothes

Yaa Veena, vara danda Manditakara- Whose hands are adorned with the perfect veena

Yaa Shweta Padmasana- One whose seat is the white lotus

Yaa Brahmachyuta Shankara Prabhrithi bhir devai,sada vandita- One who is worshiped by the Devas, praised by Brahma,Vishnu,Shiva

Saa Maam Paatu Saraswati Bhagavati Nishesha Jadyapaha- Hey Maa Saraswati, Bhagavati remove my mental inertia

Shuklaam Brahmavichaara Saara paramaam Aadhyaam Jagadvyapinim,-One Who is fair in complexion, Who is the essence of Brahman, one Who pervades the universe.

Veena Pustaka Dharineema Bhayadam Jadyandhakarapaham- Veena in one hand, Vedas in another hand, removed of ignorance and bestows fearlesness.

Haste Sphatikamalikam Vidadhateem Padmasane Samsthitam- With a rosary made of Spatika in her hand, seated on a Lotus.

Vande Tam Parmeshvareem Bhagwateem Buddhipradam Sharadam-I bow to Maa Saraswati, the source of all intellect.

10
